title: how to turn OFF the message preview when I single-click on message?
creator: alphaa10000
content:
RE: How to turn OFF message preview when I single-click on message?

When in TB message list, and selecting a message for an operation by making a single-click on the message, the selected message opens. When using CNTL key to select several messages together for an operation, each of the messages opens an individual preview pane. I have searched TB's knowledgebase, but apparently no other users have this problem.

content:

title: some subfolders not visible in thunderbird 140.10
creator: dwain0
content:
I am using Thunderbird on a Macbook Pro 13-inch mid-2012, in a POP3 account

Thunderbird 140.10 is generally running fine, except that some sub-folders are not visible in my Thunderbird email tool view. I have a hierarchical

I have tried repairing the parent folders, but that had no effect.

The corresponding .sbd files are clearly visible in ~/<user>/Library>/<profile>/Mail/Local Folders/pop-server.<account> and being backed up in Time Machine backups.

Curiously, there is a much-truncated replica of some .sbd folders in Local Folders named /<folder-name>.sbd, all dated in 2013, yet the folder was created in 2017. I presume these refer to an update of Thunderbird that left some trash, and of no consequence. These are parallel to Archives and Archives.msf files.

Please advise on how to disambiguate this structure and recover views of existing mail folders not being displayed.

title: Thunderbird Calendar Sync issues with Apple Calendar using version 150.0 (64 bit)
creator: wongi
content:
Dear Community,

I have a LAN connected desktop Windows 11 computer using Thunderbird 140.10.0esr (32-bit) synced to two different Apple calendars with no issues.

I also have two Wi-Fi connected laptop Windows 11 computers using Thunderbird 150.0 (64-bit) each synced to the same two Apple calendars above. I haven't had any sync issues with these until lately and I believe they are setup exactly the same as the desktop computer above.

For example if I attempt to "Add" a calendar entry I will get the following error message: "If you're seeing this message after snoozing or dismissing a reminder and this is for a calendar you do not want to add edit events for, you can mark this calendar as read-only to avoid such experience in future. To do so, get to the calendar properties by right clicking on this calendar in the list in the calendar or task view."

I have tried removing the calendar and adding it back but I still get the same intermittent sync issues and get these type of error messages. I have no extensions.

Not sure if this might be a software bug in the latest 150.0 (64-bit) release because 140.10esr (32-bit) appears to work fine on my desktop computer.

Any suggestions would be greatly appreciated. Many thanks.
metadata: ;useragent:Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64; rv:150.0) Gecko/20100101 Firefox/150.0;tb_version:150.0 (64 bit);os:Windows 11;kb_visits_prior:["/en-US/kb/thunderbird-makes-unrequested-connections"]
